title: glob: prefix prevents cache key collisions
description: lib.rs/evaluator.rs: raw-regex patterns are cached under their anchored regex string (e.g. '(?s)\A(?:foo)\z'); glob patterns are cached under 'glob:{pattern}'. This prefix prevents a collision where a glob pattern string happened to equal an anchored regex string. A future contributor might suggest 'simplifying' the key scheme to use the pattern directly or a shared format. The prefix is load-bearing: removing it would silently produce wrong matches when a glob pattern string coincidentally matches an anchored regex key. The prefix must stay, or the regex and glob caches must be split into separate HashMaps.
